claims: The skill claims to generate workouts but requests unrestricted Write permission, which could allow modification or deletion of unrelated files. While reading program/workout files is reasonable for its purpose, Write access without specific constraints (e.g., only allowing logs to be written to a designated directory) creates unnecessary risk. Additionally, the description includes shell command execution (!`date`, !`cat`, !`ls`) that may access system-level data beyond workout files, though this appears to be templating for display rather than core functionality. A workout generator should only need Read access to program/history files and possibly controlled Write access limited to creating new workout logs - not general Write permission.
